message:
feat(styles): add centralized animation detection with smart environment defaults

Introduce AnimationsEnabled() and ReducedMotionEnabled() as the single
source of truth for whether TUI motion effects should run. The previous
reducedMotionEnabled() only checked NTM_REDUCE_MOTION; the new logic
applies a layered decision cascade:

1. NTM_ANIMATIONS env var (explicit opt-in/opt-out, highest priority)
2. NTM_REDUCE_MOTION=1 suppresses animations
3. NTM_NO_COLOR=1 or NO_COLOR (freedesktop convention) suppresses
4. CI env var presence suppresses (headless runners)
5. TMUX or STY env var presence auto-disables (multiplexers cause
   visible tearing during full-frame ANSI repaints)
6. TERM=dumb or empty TERM suppresses
7. Falls back to terminal.SupportsTrueColor() capability check

Also adds envBool() helper for consistent truthy/falsy env var parsing
across the codebase, and exports ReducedMotionEnabled() publicly so
other packages (dashboard, tutorial, palette, components) can use it.

Tests verify auto-disable inside tmux and explicit NTM_ANIMATIONS=1
override, plus existing shimmer/pulse tests are updated to set the
full environment matrix so they pass under the stricter detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
